items.q: When a wave travels through water, what happens to the water itself?
items.correct: The water oscillates about a fixed point; the wave transfers energy, not matter.
items.misconception: The water moves along with the wave from one place to another.
items.random_wrong: The water turns into wave material and changes state.
items.why: Visually waves look like 'water moving across the surface', so students think water itself travels with the wave, missing that only energy is transferred.

items.q: What property of a sound wave determines its pitch?
items.correct: The frequency — higher frequency means higher pitch.
items.misconception: Amplitude determines pitch — a bigger wave is a higher-pitched note.
items.random_wrong: The speed of the wave determines pitch — faster waves sound higher.
items.why: Students conflate the two main wave properties: amplitude relates to volume, frequency to pitch, but they often swap them.

items.q: How do you see a non-luminous object like a book?
items.correct: Light from a source reflects off the book and enters your eyes.
items.misconception: Your eyes send out beams of vision that hit the book and let you see it.
items.random_wrong: The book emits a special invisible signal that your brain detects directly.
items.why: The 'extramission' model of vision is a stubborn childhood intuition, reinforced by phrases like 'cast your eye over' or comic-book vision rays.

items.q: Which type of wave is a sound wave?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Longitudinal. “Transverse” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: Which property of a wave is measured in hertz (Hz)?
items.why: The answer examiners want is: Frequency. “Wavelength” is the distractor this question was built around — if it tempted you, that's the exact gap to close.

items.q: A wave crest passes a buoy 5 times every second. What is the frequency, in Hz?
items.why: The real figure is 5. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
